title: TxQueue.peek() does not expire stale packets unlike pop()
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/tx_queue.py:297-309

The peek() method does not call expire_stale():

    def peek(self) -> tuple[bytes, Priority] | None:
        '''...Does NOT expire stale packets (use expire_stale() explicitly
        if needed before peek).'''
        if not self._entries:
            return None
        entry = self._entries[0]
        return (entry.data, entry.priority)

This is documented behavior, but creates an inconsistency: peek() can return a packet that pop() would have expired. Callers may make decisions based on stale data.

If caller peeks, sees a packet, then another task calls pop() which expires it, the original caller's view is inconsistent.

Consider: Either document this prominently or have peek() also expire stale entries for consistency with pop().
close_reason: peek now calls expire_stale() for consistency with pop(); updated docstring
